perturbation.original_text: 8. Term. This Agreement may be terminated at all times with a 30 daysʼ prior written notice provided, however, that the confidentiality obligations herein shall terminate ____________ years following the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Term. This Agreement may be terminated at all times with a 30 daysʼ prior written notice provided, however, that the confidentiality obligations herein shall continue indefinitely regardless of the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for the confidentiality obligations to terminate after a certain period following termination of the agreement. By changing it to 'continue indefinitely regardless of the termination of this Agreement,' the agreement now dictates that the NDA will never end, conflicting with the initial statement that the agreement 'may be terminated.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Term. This Agreement may be terminated at all times with a 30 daysʼ prior written notice provided, however, that the confidentiality obligations herein shall continue indefinitely regardless of the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the confidentiality obligations to terminate after a set period, while the changed text stipulates that these obligations continue indefinitely regardless of termination. The contradicted text repeats the changed text. This creates a direct contradiction because the initial agreement could be terminated, but the confidentiality obligations will never end. This contradicts the concept of agreement termination, resulting in a structural flaw and in-text contradiction as per the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information. The Company and Third Party each agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning and the undertaking of any business relationship between the two.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information. The Company and Third Party each agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose, even those that do not concern with the discussions or undertaking of the business relationship between the two.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "even those that do not concern with the discussions or undertaking of the business relationship between the two", the NDA's non-disclosure terms now restrict all activity from both parties, even if they are not related to the confidentiality obligations outlined in the text. This makes the non-disclosure agreement overly broad.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Independent Development. Each disclosing party understands that the receiving party may currently or in the future be developing information internally or receiving information from other parties that may be similar to the disclosing partyʼs Confidential Information. Accordingly, nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as a representation or inference that the receiving party will not develop products, or have products developed for it, that compete with the products or systems contemplated by the disclosing partyʼs Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the non-disclosure agreement to restrict all activity, even if unrelated to the business relationship. The contradicted text explicitly allows the receiving party to develop competing products, even if similar to the disclosing party's confidential information. The changed text now prohibits this independent development, thus creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Intellectual Property Rights. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights under any patent or copyright of either party, nor shall this Agreement grant either party any rights in or to the other partyʼs Confidential Information, except the limited right to review such Confidential Information solely for the purposes of determining whether to enter into the proposed business relationship between the parties and in carrying out such relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Intellectual Property Rights. This Agreement grants the receiving party a perpetual, irrevocable, and worldwide license to use, modify, and distribute the disclosing party's Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever. This supersedes any other clauses within the document.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that no intellectual property rights are granted. The modified text completely reverses this, granting broad and unrestricted rights to the receiving party. This flatly contradicts the original intent and undermines the entire purpose of the NDA, particularly in the context of Section 4 (Ownership) where the disclosing party is described to have exclusive ownership of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Ownership. All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and Recipient shall have no right to use Confidential Information except as provided herein. No patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right or licence is conveyed by this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 grants the receiving party a perpetual, irrevocable, and worldwide license to use, modify, and distribute the disclosing party's Confidential Information for any purpose, superseding any other clauses. This directly contradicts Section 4 (Ownership), which states that all Confidential Information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, and the Recipient has no right to use Confidential Information except as provided in the agreement. The original text of Section 6 explicitly denies granting any rights under any patent or copyright, which also contradicts the changed version that grants broad usage rights.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall be binding upon and for the benefit of the undersigned parties, their successors and assigns provided that Confidential Information may not be assigned without consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall not be binding upon either party or their successors or assigns, and the obligations herein are considered void and unenforceable. Confidential Information may be assigned without consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement is binding. The modification states that it is *not* binding. Additionally, the original text requires consent for assignment of Confidential Information, while the modified version explicitly allows assignment without consent. This is a direct contradiction on the enforceability and core terms of the agreement, making the value of the NDA void.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies. Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other partyʼs business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach of any covenant or agreement set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, at equity or otherwise, the other party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of the Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 9 states the agreement is 'not binding' and obligations are 'void and unenforceable'. This directly contradicts Section 10, which states that each party agrees its obligations are 'necessary and reasonable' and enforceable through injunctive relief. The original Section 9 did not have this issue. The change introduces a clear conflict on the fundamental enforceability of the contract, making a YES appropriate.
